era: Seattle, 2012-present. Electronic duo of Harrison Mills and Clayton Knight, formed at Western Washington University (Bellingham, WA). One of the biggest electronic acts to emerge from the PNW. Grammy-nominated. Coachella headliners. Cinematic, euphoric electronic music.

location.origin: Bellingham, Washington. Formed at Western Washington University.
location.formative: Western Washington University campus. Bellingham music community. Self-released debut while still in college.
location.career: ODESZA -- electronic duo. Harrison Mills and Clayton Knight. Formed at WWU in Bellingham, relocated to Seattle. Counter Records. Albums: Summer's Gone (2012), In Return (2014), A Moment Apart (2017, Grammy-nominated), The Last Goodbye (2022). Coachella headliners. Electronic at Genre Stage 77. E=5.

labels: Counter Records (primary label for major releases)

members.name: Harrison Mills
members.note: Co-founder. Met Clayton Knight at Western Washington University in Bellingham.

members.name: Clayton Knight
members.note: Co-founder. Met Harrison Mills at WWU. Both producers who bonded over electronic music.

associated_musicians.name: Death Cab for Cutie
associated_musicians.note: Both formed at Western Washington University in Bellingham. Death Cab launched indie rock; ODESZA launched electronic music. Different genres, different decades, same pipeline.

associated_musicians.name: The Postal Service
associated_musicians.note: Both electronic, both massive reach. Postal Service is indie-electronic crossover; ODESZA is cinematic electronic. Both E=5-scale visibility.

associated_musicians.name: Chong the Nomad
associated_musicians.note: Both electronic, both Seattle. Chong is beat-driven production; ODESZA is cinematic builds. Different scales, shared electronic lineage.

associated_musicians.name: Hovercraft
associated_musicians.note: Opposite ends of Seattle's electronic spectrum. Hovercraft is drone/experimental; ODESZA is euphoric and pop-accessible.

associated_musicians.name: Sara Bareilles
associated_musicians.note: Both E=5, both arena-scale. Bareilles in pop/piano; ODESZA in electronic. Same energy tier and commercial magnitude.

theory_nodes.concept: The euphoric build (ninety-sixth acoustic element -- the sonic architecture of rising tension through layered synthesizers, vocal samples building intensity, textural accumulation, harmonic suspension, building to a massive crescendo and release; cinematic electronic construction; the build IS the composition; collective experience; festival-scale)
examples: ODESZA (degree 95) -- cinematic electronic architecture. Tension, suspension, release. The euphoric build.
Contrast with the producer's palette (degree 94, Erik Blood) -- experimental studio exploration. The euphoric build is structured and purpose-built for collective experience.
Contrast with the shimmer veil (degree 93, NAVVI) -- intimate reverb architecture. The euphoric build is massive-scale, arena-designed.
The engine adds the sound of collective euphoria. Festival-scale electronic architecture.
theory_nodes.description: The euphoric build is the 96th acoustic element. Cinematic electronic architecture. Rising tension, layered synths, vocal samples, massive release. Festival-scale collective experience.

ancestors: The producer's palette (degree 94, Erik Blood) -- studio-as-instrument, experimental. The euphoric build is structured, purposeful.
The shimmer veil (degree 93, NAVVI) -- reverb-as-architecture, intimate. The euphoric build is massive and euphoric.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-six acoustic elements across ninety-five degrees. Track element 97 at degree 96.

theory_nodes.concept: The Bellingham pipeline (ninety-sixth artist-city pattern -- a university town incubates artists who then launch into Seattle and the wider world; specifically Western Washington University in Bellingham, WA; Death Cab for Cutie (degree 58) and ODESZA (degree 95) both formed at WWU; different genres, different decades, same geographic pipeline)
examples: Death Cab for Cutie (degree 58) -- indie rock from Bellingham/WWU. Launched into Seattle and the world.
ODESZA (degree 95) -- electronic from Bellingham/WWU. Same pipeline, different genre, different decade.
The pipeline mechanics: cheap rent, university community, proximity to Seattle, independent cultural identity.
theory_nodes.description: The Bellingham pipeline is the 96th artist-city pattern. WWU incubates artists. Death Cab (58) and ODESZA (95). Same origin, different genres.

ancestors: The cross-genre bridge (degree 94, Erik Blood) -- connecting existing scenes. The Bellingham pipeline is about geographic origin and launch trajectory.
The intimate machine (degree 93, NAVVI) -- about scale. The Bellingham pipeline is about geography and institutional incubation.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-six artist-city patterns across ninety-five degrees. Track pattern 97 at degree 96.

theory_nodes.concept: Electronic at Genre Stage 77 -- cinematic electronic production, euphoric builds, vocal samples, festival-scale sound design, layered synths, massive drops; distinguished from Electronic/Experimental (94) and Electronic Pop (86)
examples: Distinguished from Electronic/Experimental (Erik Blood, 94) -- ODESZA is structured, euphoric, pop-accessible.
Distinguished from Electronic Pop (Postal Service, 86) -- ODESZA is festival-scale, instrumental-forward.
Sixth electronic-adjacent genre in ten degrees (86, 90, 91, 93, 94, 95).
theory_nodes.description: Electronic at Genre Stage 77. Cinematic electronic. Festival-scale. Euphoric builds. Six electronic entries in ten degrees.

ancestors: Electronic/Experimental (degree 94) at Genre Stage 76. Genre stages continue.
Electronic Pop (degree 86, Postal Service). Different electronic approach.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 77 at degree 95. Track at 96.

theory_nodes.concept: E=5 at degree 95 -- Coachella headliner, Grammy-nominated, arena-headlining tours, Counter Records; one of the biggest electronic acts from the PNW; massive commercial success
examples: ODESZA (degree 95) -- E=5. Coachella headliner. Grammy-nominated. Arena tours.
E=5 ties Sara Bareilles (81) at the energy ceiling.
After E=4 at degree 94 (Erik Blood), energy rises to maximum at 95.
theory_nodes.description: E=5 at degree 95. Arena-scale. Coachella headliner. Grammy-nominated. Counter Records. Maximum energy.

ancestors: E=4 at degree 94 (Erik Blood). Energy rises from 4 to 5.

cross_references.relationship: BELLINGHAM/WWU ORIGIN! Both formed at Western Washington University. Death Cab launched indie rock; ODESZA launched electronic music. Different genres, different decades, same pipeline. The two most commercially successful acts from Bellingham.
Electronic connection. Both electronic, both massive reach. Postal Service is indie-electronic crossover; ODESZA is cinematic electronic. Both E=5-scale visibility through electronic music.
Electronic Seattle parallel. Both electronic, both Seattle. Chong is beat-driven production; ODESZA is cinematic builds. Different scales, shared electronic lineage.
Electronic spectrum. Opposite ends of Seattle's electronic spectrum. Hovercraft is drone/experimental; ODESZA is euphoric, structured, pop-accessible.
E=5 parallel. Both E=5, both arena-scale. Bareilles in pop/piano; ODESZA in electronic. Different genres, same energy tier and commercial magnitude.
